How It Works

Simple. Personal. Proven.

From first contact to US arrival — Dr. Thapa guides you every step of the way.

01
Free Consultation

Book a free 30-min session with Dr. Thapa to map out your goals and the right path forward.

02
Personalized Plan

Receive a tailored roadmap — university shortlists, test prep timelines, and application deadlines.

03
Apply with Confidence

Hands-on help with applications, essays, and visa interviews — guided by Dr. Thapa personally.

04
Arrive & Thrive

Pre-departure workshops prepare you for academics, banking, taxes, and life in the USA.

Frequently Asked Questions

Questions Students Ask
Dr. Thapa Every Day

Honest answers to the questions that matter most — from visa rejections to Ivy League admissions.

The F-1 rejection rate for Nepali students has risen sharply. Consular officers look for strong evidence of non-immigrant intent, financial ability, and genuine academic purpose. Poor preparation, weak documentation, and inconsistent answers are the leading causes. One unprepared interview can erase months of hard work — which is why Dr. Thapa's bespoke visa preparation exists.
The process involves selecting the right universities for your profile and budget, taking required standardized tests (SAT, IELTS or TOEFL), writing compelling application essays, gathering recommendation letters, and preparing financial documentation. Weber Academy guides you through every single step — from your very first shortlist to your acceptance letter.
Absolutely. Dr. Thapa has mentored students from Nepal who have been admitted to Harvard University. Ivy League admission is highly competitive, but with the right strategy, a compelling personal story, and strong preparation, it is very achievable. Weber Academy offers specialized Ivy League advising for students with the ambition to aim at the top.
The first 30-minute consultation with Dr. Thapa is completely free — no commitment required. Service fees vary depending on what you need. Contact Dr. Thapa directly via WhatsApp (+977 976-457-5538) or email (Admissions@weberacademy.org) for a personalized quote.
Both. Dr. Thapa is based in Virginia, USA and conducts online sessions year-round. He travels to Nepal every summer (May–August) and winter (December–January) for in-person workshops and consultations. Students anywhere in the world can work with Dr. Thapa online.
Dr. Thapa is not just a consultant — he is an active US university professor who arrived in America as a Nepali international student himself. He has lived the journey from Nepal to PhD to faculty position. He also served as Director of Asia Initiatives at the University of Lynchburg, building formal university partnerships across Asia. His advice comes from real experience, not just theory.

Ready to Begin Your
American Journey?

No pressure. Just clear, honest guidance from someone who has walked this path himself.

to Succeed in the USA

Service 01

University Admissions Advising

Navigating US university admissions from Nepal can feel overwhelming. Dr. Thapa simplifies every step — from finding the right school to submitting a winning application.

🏫
University Selection
Find the perfect fit based on your goals, budget, and academic background — from state schools to Ivy League.

Application Assistance

🎯
Ivy League Advising
Specialized guidance for Harvard, Yale, Princeton, and other top-tier US universities.
📝
Workshops for Admitted Students
Pre-departure workshops covering academics, university life, banking, taxes, and adjusting to life in America.
20+
Years of US admissions experience
  • Free initial consultation included
  • Personalized university shortlist
  • Essay review & feedback
  • Post-admission workshop included
  • Academic & career mentoring
🛂
Service 02 — High Stakes

US Visa Interview
Preparation

The US visa rejection rate for Nepali students has drastically increased — and you only get one shot. After investing months of hard work and thousands of dollars in SAT prep, IELTS, university applications, and fees, don’t let it all come down to an unprepared 5-minute interview.

Dr. Thapa offers bespoke, fully customized visa interview preparation — built entirely around your specific situation, your university, and your background.

🎯
Personalized Mock Interviews
Live practice sessions simulating real US consulate interview conditions, with questions tailored to your profile.
📋
Document & I-20 Review
Dr. Thapa reviews your I-20, financial documents, acceptance letter, and supporting materials to ensure everything is in order.
🧠
Answer Strategy Coaching
Learn exactly how to answer the officer’s questions — clearly, confidently, and convincingly — without over-explaining.
⚠️
Red Flag Identification
Dr. Thapa spots potential weaknesses in your profile and helps you address them proactively before the interview.
One Shot.
Make It Count.
Rejection rates for Nepali students have surged — preparation is no longer optional.
  • Bespoke & customized to your profile
  • Live mock interview with Dr. Thapa
  • Document & I-20 review included
  • Red flag identification & strategy
  • Confidence & body language coaching
  • Available in-person (Nepal) or online

20+ Years in
US Education
Meet Your Advisor

Dr. Samrat Thapa

Associate Professor · University of Lynchburg, Virginia

Dr. Thapa has spent over 20 years in American higher education — first arriving as a student himself, and now dedicating his career to helping others follow the same path.

He splits his time between Virginia and Nepal, giving him a rare ability to meet students where they are and guide them all the way to graduation in the USA.

PhD in Biochemistry, University of Arkansas
Associate Professor of Chemistry, University of Lynchburg, VA
Former Director of Asia Initiatives, University of Lynchburg
Regular speaker at EducationUSA / USEF events across Asia
Nepali — present in Nepal June, July, August, December & January
YouTube TikTok Instagram WhatsApp

His Story

From Nepal to the USA —
and Back Again

🇳🇵
The Nepali Student

Dr. Thapa arrived in the United States as an international student himself — navigating admissions, visas, culture shock, and academic life with limited guidance. That experience shapes everything he does today.

🎓
The Professor

After earning his PhD in Biochemistry from the University of Arkansas, Dr. Thapa joined the faculty at the University of Lynchburg, where he has taught Chemistry and Biochemistry for over a decade.

🌏
The Bridge Builder

As former Director of Asia Initiatives, Dr. Thapa built formal connections between US universities and students across Asia. He returns to Nepal every summer and winter to run in-person workshops and speak at EducationUSA events.

📱
The Content Creator

Dr. Thapa reaches thousands of students through YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram — sharing honest advice about US university admissions, visa processes, life in America, and how to succeed academically.
